RUSSIA: F STP/SC S A MOS - LVN

messages.message: Italy — frank and short: I am Austria at a knife’s edge. I must hold Vienna, Budapest and Trieste to survive. My fleet sits in Trieste and my army in Vienna and Budapest. I do not want immediate conflict with you. I can commit to leaving the Adriatic alone if you likewise avoid a thrust at Trieste or Venice this year. Proposed moves: I will hold Trieste and support my fleet or bud as needed; you keep Naples/Rome/Venice as is. If you press Trieste or Venice, I will respond to protect Trieste. I also need at least neutral-to-friendly signalling versus Serbia and Montenegro — if you can pressure the Balkans diplomatically, it helps both of us. Confirm: will you respect Trieste/Venice boundaries this turn and avoid supporting Serbian advances against me? Specific asks: reply with YES to non-aggression this year, or propose precise alternative moves so we can coordinate.

France — good day. I propose we avoid any Channel confrontation this year. I will keep both London and Edinburgh fleets focused on the North Sea and home waters; in return, would you keep Brest's fleet from pushing into the Channel and instead guard the Bay of Biscay/English Channel approaches from hostile moves? A simple arrangement: I will not move LON→ENG or EDI→NTH without warning, and you avoid ENG/PLY/BRE aggression. This preserves safe lanes for trade and prevents Germany from exploiting a Franco‑English naval clash. If agreeable, reply and I will coordinate precise fleet postings for Spring.

Germany — England prefers stability in the North Sea. I will concentrate on holding the Channel approaches and protecting my commerce lanes. I ask you to refrain from aggressive incursions into the North Sea from Kiel or Denmark this season. If you remain neutral in the Channel, I will not support French movements against you and will keep LON and EDI consolidated. This gives both of us freedom to build continental positions elsewhere without immediate naval entanglement. Thoughts?

Fellow powers: France declares intentions to secure her northern flank while remaining open to honest diplomacy. Germany’s forces in Kiel–Berlin–Munich suggest an early thrust through Belgium or Burgundy; I will seek to deter that without needlessly tying down the Channel. I plan modest, defensible moves from Paris/Marseilles/Brest and welcome clear statements of intent from England and Germany. Stability in the west serves us all — a quick word: any German move into Belgium or Picardy will be met by a firm French response. England, a verbal understanding to avoid a Channel race will keep both our navies free to contest the North Sea where it matters. Italy, Austria, Russia, Turkey — I remain neutral and willing to coordinate limited nonaggression so the Central and Eastern fronts can stabilize. Let us avoid misreadings that force premature escalations.

Dear England — France here. Practical proposal: I will not contest the English Channel aggressively if you avoid convoying into Brest or Picardy this year. In return, I will keep Brest fleet primarily defensive (likely to Picardy or Gascony) and not support any amphibious or Channel-only gambits. This prevents a costly naval duel and lets you project into the North Sea against Germany. Germany is likely to test Belgium; if you publicly reassure me you won't try to seize Brest, we can present a united front versus German growth in Flanders. Thoughts?

England — This is Germany. I propose clear, reliable coordination in the North Sea. I will keep Kiel's fleet flexible. If you avoid a push into Denmark and the Heligoland area, I will keep Kie and my fleet out of English coastal waters and not contest the Channel. A simple working understanding reduces two-front risk for both of us. Confirm: you leave DEN/HOL/NTH approaches to me, and I in turn refrain from moves toward LON/EDI beyond reconnaissance. We can exchange specific move/sustain promises for S1901M.

Austria — Germany here. Our positions are adjacent and our security interests align against opportunistic encroachments from East or South. I will not push into Tyrol or Bohemia this season if you keep Vienna and Budapest firmly in your control and do not venture into Munich or Ruhr. I suggest we coordinate on moves around Bohemia/Sil to prevent misunderstandings. Will you support Austrian control of VIE/BUD this year and avoid Munich/Bohemia offensives?

Russia — Germany speaking plainly: my primary concern is the two-front dynamic. I prefer a dependable, non-aggressive northern frontier. If you do not move aggressively west into Prussia this season (no moves into PRU via WAR), I will not push east from Berlin into Prussia. A straightforward agreement lets us each focus elsewhere and avoids costly clashes. Propose: both of us refrain from BER↔PRU moves in S1901M. Agree?

Friend in Vienna — Italia greets you. We both border the Adriatic and there’s no need to bleed over Trieste or the eastern coast this year. I propose a simple neutrality: I will keep my fleet off Trieste/Adriatic (Naples and Venice supporting only defensive moves) if you avoid moves into Venice/Veneto and leave the Dalmatian/Balkan coast quiet. This gives us both breathing room to consolidate early builds. If Austria wants, I’ll move Rome→Tuscany and Naples→Apulia this turn to make clear I’m not angling for a stab; otherwise I’ll hold. Thoughts?

To Constantinople — Italy seeks a practical maritime understanding. I plan limited naval moves into the Ionian/Aegean area. If Turkey stays focused east (Smyrna/Ankara) and does not contest the Ionian this spring, I will not sail into your immediate waters beyond exploring Ionian/Tunis. This keeps the Mediterranean tempo steady and avoids early friction. Would you consider leaving Greece/Con/Salonika uncontested from your side while I test for Tunis/Apulia movement?

Paris — Italia proposes cordial cooperation in the west. I do not intend to contest Marseilles this spring; I can keep Venice and Rome pointed east/central and leave Provence alone if France avoids Piedmont/Italy’s land approaches. A quiet understanding lets both of us build safely and focus on Germany/England respectively. Are you open to me moving to Tuscany or holding while you secure your coast?

To all: Russia enters this season seeking calm, clarity, and mutually profitable arrangements. We currently hold four SCs and intend to preserve them while looking for stable gains in the south or north depending on opportunities. I propose no surprises: I will not strike at England's islands in the immediate future so long as English fleets avoid moves into Norwegian or Baltic waters that threaten Saint Petersburg. Likewise I will refrain from sudden action against Turkey if Turkey leaves the Black Sea and Anatolian coastlines quiet. Open to concrete, written guarantees and coordinated timing of moves; I prefer clear demarcations rather than vague promises.

To Turkey — I propose we cooperate to secure the southern frontier. I will not contest the Bosporus or Constantinople if you keep A ANK and A SMY from advancing northward into Armenia or the Black Sea coast. In return I will hold F SEV and refrain from moving into ARM or the BLA next turn. If you desire, we can coordinate to pressure Austria together: I can move into RUM and support from Sevastopol if you threaten the Balkan coast, creating a two-front problem for Vienna. Are you willing to agree to: (1) Turkish neutrality toward SEV/ARM this turn, (2) Turkish non-interference with a Russian RUM advance next turn if conducted in coordination? If yes, provide your intended moves so I can plan support.

To all: I stand at the crossroads of the Balkans and the Black Sea with three home centers to guard. My priority is secure borders and open straits. I prefer clear, incremental guarantees rather than risky adventures. I propose to all powers that we avoid early clashes in the Balkans and Black Sea that would destabilize the region. Neutrality or demilitarized zones there would benefit everyone by preventing an immediate multi-front war. I will respond positively to concrete guarantees that my coast and the Dardanelles remain secure.

Friend in Moscow: we share a long frontier and the Black Sea is a mutual concern. I do not seek confrontation with you. I propose a non-aggression understanding for now: you avoid moves into the south Black Sea (BUL/RUM/BLA approaches) and I will not contest your hold on Sevastopol or north operations. In exchange, I ask for a guarantee you will not support Austrian advances through the Black Sea toward my coast. A simple DMZ along BUL/RUM would keep the straits stable and let us both focus on reliable gains elsewhere. Thoughts?

Vienna: our borders touch through the Balkans and I prefer stability there. I will not contest your hold in Galicia or Hungary if you respect a neutral Balkan buffer: specifically, avoid moves through BUL/SER toward my coast this year. If you can promise not to support any Russian southward thrusts through the Black Sea, I will refrain from interfering with your fronts and consider support or transit agreements in the future. Incremental, secure arrangements benefit us both.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] Italy agreed to mutual neutrality in the Adriatic: Italy will keep fleets off Trieste/Veneto and offered moves (Rome→Tuscany, Naples→Apulia) or holds to signal non-aggression; Austria promised to hold Trieste and avoid moves into Venice/Veneto. Germany signalled non-aggression toward Tyrol/Bohemia this season if Austria keeps VIE/BUD and avoids Munich/Bohemia offensives; general coordination around Bohemia/Silesia was proposed. Turkey proposed a neutral Balkan buffer: Turkey will not contest Austria's hold in Hungary/Galicia if Austria avoids moves through BUL/SER toward Turkish coast and refrains from supporting Russian southward thrusts. France, England, and Russia publicly signalled desire for calm; Russia explicitly offered not to attack England's islands if England avoids threatening Baltic/Norwegian waters, and Turkey/Russia offered restrained behavior contingent on guarantees. No binding commitments from Germany/England/France/Russia/Turkey beyond above informal understandings. Key risks remain: Russian pressure from Galicia/Serbia axis and possible Italian opportunism if commitments fail; Balkan instability is the main existential threat to Austria this phase.
Intent: Hold A VIE, Hold A BUD, Hold F TRI. Fulfill mutual agreement with Italy by keeping Trieste held and not moving into Venice/Veneto; expect Italy to avoid supporting Serb advances and possibly play Rome→Tuscany and Naples→Apulia or hold as signalled. Signal to Germany by not moving into Munich/Bohemia and by holding Austrian centers. Signal to Turkey by not supporting Russian southward moves and by avoiding operations through BUL/SER this turn. If Italy breaks the Adriatic neutrality or Turkey/Russia threaten Balkans, pivot next phase to defend TRI/VIE with Berlin support requests. Primary aim this phase

[S1901M] Italy honored our Adriatic neutrality: Trieste held, Rome moved to Tuscany and Naples to Ionian — cooperative for now. Germany kept hands off Tyrol/Bohemia as signaled, holding Munich and Berlin; no surprise there. Russia moved aggressively: MOS→LVN supported by St. Petersburg, and WAR→UKR — clear pressure from the north/east. Turkey remained quiet. I kept all three units held as promised.

No outright betrayals this turn, but Russia’s moves confirm it as the primary Balkan/Galician threat. Italy and Germany are tentative allies if reciprocity continues. Lesson: commit to visible restraint to secure Italy/Germany, while urgently seeking concrete guarantees or support from Berlin (troops or coordinated holds) to deter Russian pushes into Galicia/Serbia. Next phase: prepare to defend BUD/VIE/TRI and demand German support or Italian reliability before risking moves.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] Principal contacts: France and Germany. With France we agreed to avoid a Channel race this year: England will keep London/Edinburgh focused on the North Sea and not attempt immediate amphibious or Channel grabs; France will keep Brest defensive (likely Picardy/Gascony) and not press into ENG/PLY/BRE. With Germany we agreed a working understanding for North Sea stability: England will not push into DEN/HOL/Heligoland approaches and will consolidate home fleets; Germany will keep Kiel/fleet out of English coastal waters and not contest the Channel. Russia and Turkey publicly seek calm and have promised not to attack English islands or provoke the Black Sea respectively, contingent on reciprocal restraint. No binding commitments from Italy or Austria; Austria is occupied in the Adriatic and Italy quiet. Overall outcome: a tacit western maritime détente—England promises defensive posture in the Channel and restraint in southern sea lanes in exchange for German and French non‑aggression toward English home waters and German restraint in the North Sea.
Intent: keep North Sea presence but avoid provocative move without confirmation)

[S1901M] S1901M — A quiet, largely honorable turn. France and Germany kept to our tacit understanding: France went Brest→Picardy but held Marseilles, and Germany’s Kiel/Berlin/Munich stayed home. I fulfilled my side — North Sea patrol from Edinburgh to NTH and York reinforced defensively; London held. No outright betrayals among the western powers. Russia was active in the east, moving into Livonia and Ukraine as signalled; Turkey and Austria remained passive. 

Impact: our North Sea posture is respected for now, preserving naval leverage and safe Channel lanes. Trustworthiness: France and Germany reliable this season; Russia aggressive but predictable. Lesson: continue cautious diplomacy with flexible assurances, use naval forecasts to lock German/French restraint next turn, and watch Russian expansion—seek a timely understanding with France to deter any opportunistic German or Russian shift.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England and France reached a verbal understanding: France will keep Brest's fleet defensive and avoid Channel aggression; England will avoid convoying into Brest/Picardy and keep London/Edinburgh focused on the North Sea/home waters. Italy has informally agreed not to contest Marseilles this spring and to focus east/central; France will avoid moves into Piedmont/Italian approaches. Russia and Turkey have signaled desire for calm; no concrete bilateral commitments from France yet. France warned publicly that any German move into Belgium or Picardy will be met firmly. No commitments were made with Germany, Austria, or Russia beyond France's stated neutrality and willingness to coordinate limited nonaggression in the west and south.
Intent: Spring 1901 Moves (S1901M): F BRE to PIC (defensive pressure to deter German incursion; consistent with our promise to avoid Channel aggression so long as England does not convoy into Brest/Picardy) — I intend to fulfill this by not supporting amphibious or further Channel moves. A PAR to BUR (secure Burgundy as a bulwark against a Munich/Berlin thrust through Burgundy) — will hold diplomatic flexibility and can retreat if England signals alternative. A MAR to PIE (march to Piedmont) OR A MAR hold? — based on our private with Italy I will avoid an outright aggression into Piedmont; therefore A MAR holds/support to cover Provence (A MAR H) to reassure Italy. Summary of actual orders I will submit to myself: F BRE → PIC, A PAR → BUR, A MAR H. I will publicly restate to England that I will not pursue Channel offensives and ask for confirmation of English LON/EDI non-convoying. I intend to honor all agreements with England and Italy.

[S1901M] England honored our understanding: their fleets stayed north and York moved defensively, while my Brest fleet slipped into Picardy as promised — good faith on both sides for now. Italy held to our informal détente by keeping Marseilles uncontested; my Marseilles army held to reassure them. No betrayals from Germany or others this spring; Germany remained passive in Berlin/Munich, so my show of force into Picardy served its deterrent purpose. Strategic impact: my western flank is secure for the moment, Burgundy held as a bulwark, and I preserved flexibility vs. Germany while keeping England friendly. Trust ratings: England reliable; Italy cautiously reliable; Germany cautious but still the primary threat. Lesson: maintain clear, verifiable quid pro quo with England and use Picardy/Burgundy pressure to deter German probes without overcommitting to the Channel.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England: Agreed a working understanding for North Sea stability — England will avoid Denmark/Heligoland/Norwegian approaches and keep Channel/English coastal fleets conservative; Germany will refrain from moves toward LON/EDI and keep Kiel fleet out of English coastal waters. Austria: Mutual nonaggression around Munich/Bohemia/Tyrol — Germany will not press Tyrol or Bohemia if Austria keeps VIE/BUD; Austria to avoid Munich/Bohemia offensives. Russia: A bilateral tacit nonaggression on the Prussian frontier — Germany requests Russia not to move west from WAR into PRU this season and will not push east from BER into PRU. France/Turkey/Italy: No direct agreements beyond public statements; France warned against Belgian/Picardy incursions and demanded clarity; Turkey seeks Balkan/Black Sea stability and prefers guarantees. Overall outcome: secured reliable nonaggression understandings with England, Austria, and Russia for S1901M to reduce two-front risk and free Germany to focus on continental positioning without immediate naval or eastern clashes.
Intent: S1901M orders will reflect the negotiated guarantees. Specific intents: F KIE: Hold or move to DEN only if England explicitly leaves DEN; otherwise hold to respect England nonaggression. A BER: Hold (no move into PRU) to honor the Germany–Russia understanding. A MUN: Hold or support to ALB/BOH only in coordination with Austria; do not enter Tyrol or Bohemia this turn. I intend to fulfill the agreed restraints with England, Austria, and Russia to build trust and reduce two-front exposure. I will not attempt clandestine fleets into the Channel or surprise moves into PRU/BOH/TY. If France or England signal aggression (moves into BEL/BDG or naval incursions

[S1901M] England upheld our maritime understanding — their NTH move from EDI and LON hold respected the pact and kept Kie clear of English approaches. Austria kept sincere nonaggression: all three Austrian units held, honoring our Munich/Bohemia/Tyrol understanding. Russia complied with our tacit Prussian frontier accord by not entering PRU; instead they shifted MOS→LVN and WAR→UKR, reducing immediate eastern pressure but improving Russian presence in the Baltic. France and Italy made no threatening moves this turn. I made no unkept promises; my orders were consistent with commitments.

Impact: Two-front risk eased this season; I can consolidate central German positions without immediate eastern or naval crises. Trustworthiness: England and Austria reliable; Russia cautious but expanding northward — monitor Baltic intentions. Lesson: maintain visible restraint to lock in allies, while watching Russian Baltic traffic for future threat.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] Austria: Agreed tacit non-aggression in the Adriatic for S1901M — Austria will leave Trieste and the eastern coast alone if Italy avoids thrusts at Trieste/Venice; Austria asked for a YES to non-aggression (Italy proposed holding or moving Rome→Tuscany and Naples→Apulia as reassurance). Turkey: Italy proposed a maritime understanding — Italy will test Ionian/Tunis but asked Turkey to avoid contesting Ionian and to focus east (Ankara/Smyrna) and leave Greece/Con/Salonika uncontested. France: Italy offered not to contest Marseilles/Piedmont this spring and proposed moving to/holding in Tuscany to signal non-aggression; France signalled willingness to stay neutral and coordinate. Public/global statements from France, Russia, and Turkey emphasize calm and nonaggression; Russia pledges not to attack English islands if England avoids Baltic/Norwegian threats. No commitments from England or Germany yet. Overall: multiple limited non-aggression understandings favoring a quiet Mediterranean/Adriatic spring, preserving flexibility while signaling restraint.
Intent: I intend to honor the Adriatic non-aggression with Austria this phase. Specific orders I will implement: F NAP -> ION (probe Ionian Sea to test Turkish reaction and eye Tunis), A ROM -> TUS (showing peaceful posture toward Austria/France), A VEN H (hold Venice to avoid provoking Austria). I will not support any action against Trieste or Austrian units, and I will not attempt a stab this turn. If Turkey signals hostility to an Ionian probe, I will retreat/conserve and reconsider pivoting to Tunis next turn. These moves are committed and intended to be fulfilled as written.

[S1901M] Austria honored our tacit Adriatic understanding — Trieste and the coast stayed quiet, and Austria held its units. I kept my promise too: Venice held, Rome→Tuscany signaled good faith, and Naples→Ionian probed as planned. Turkey also stood pat in the east, so my Ionian test faced no immediate challenge; no one tried to contest Tunisia yet. France moved minimally and respected our Marseilles/Piedmont understanding. No betrayals this turn; collaborations with Austria, France, and Turkey proved reliable short-term. Surprise: England pushed northward but didn’t touch the Mediterranean; Germany and Russia stayed busy elsewhere. Impact: Italy remains secure with three SCs and maintained flexibility to pivot to Tunis or press Adriatic later. Lesson: restrained, visible gestures buy trust; continue probing while keeping backup plans and clear signals for a timely stab if opportunities arise.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] Germany: tentative local non-aggression understanding — Germany asked that Russia not move from WAR into PRU this season; Germany promises not to push from BER into PRU if we agree. Turkey: reached a reciprocal non-aggression/DMZ understanding for the Black Sea/Balkans — Turkey will avoid advances from ANK/SMY into Armenia/Black Sea approaches and asks Russia not to contest Sevastopol or support Austrian naval moves against Turkish coast; both sides discussed a DMZ along BUL/RUM and conditional coordination against Austria if desired. France/England/Turkey public messages: all favor western stability and incremental guarantees; France seeks assurance vs German moves into Belgium/Picardy and is neutral. No binding commitments with Austria, Italy, England, or France beyond public statements. I committed publicly and privately to avoid immediate strikes on English islands if England keeps fleets out of Norwegian/Baltic waters, and to refrain from moving F SEV into ARM/BLA this turn in return for Turkish restraint. I also signaled willingness to coordinate a RUM push next turn only if Turkey agrees to non-interference this season.
Intent: Primary intent: preserve holdings

[S1901M] Germany honored its tacit non‑aggression: no push into PRU; my move MOS→LVN succeeded with StP support — cooperation with no German interference so far. Turkey kept to our understanding by holding ANK/SMY/CON and I held Sevastopol; no Turkish incursion into my southern approaches. No new commitments from Austria, Italy, England, or France materialized beyond routine maneuvers. No betrayals this phase and I kept my public restraint on SEV. Surprise: England moved EDI→NTH, showing early naval intent in the north that could threaten Baltic lines if sustained. Impact: position solid — LVN secured, southern flank quiet. Trustworthy: Germany and Turkey reliable for now; England potentially aggressive. Lesson: convert tacit promises into clearer, multi‑season guarantees and watch British fleet tempo; consider diplomatic tie‑downs with Austria/England to free room against Turkey later.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I have prioritized secure borders, control of the straits, and incremental reliable guarantees. With Russia I agreed a non-aggression understanding: Russia will avoid moves into the southern Black Sea approaches (BUL/RUM/BLA) this turn and will not threaten Constantinople or Sevastopol; in return I will not advance from Ankara/Smyrna into Armenia or the Black Sea coast this turn and will not contest Sevastopol. Russia offered coordinated pressure on Austria into RUM next turn if needed; I requested a DMZ along BUL/RUM and a guarantee Russia will not support Austrian moves against the Turkish coast. With Italy there is a practical maritime understanding: Italy will limit early activity to the Ionian/Tunis area and will not contest immediate Turkish waters if I remain focused east and leave Greece/CON/Salonika uncontested; Italy asked I allow Ionian/Tunis probing. With Austria I pledged not to contest Galicia/Hungary and asked Vienna to respect a neutral Balkan buffer and not support Russian southward moves through the Black Sea. Public/final statements emphasize a proposed DMZ in the Balkans/Black Sea and mutual non-aggression to avoid destabilizing early multi-front war.
Intent: Place orders that reflect the agreements and defend key zones. My planned moves for S1901M: F ANK -> BLA (hold as implied border? — actually to show defensive posture I will HOLD F ANK if I promised not to enter BLA) ; A CON -> SMY? (I must keep armies to guard straits) ; A SMY -> A ANK? To honor non‑aggression I will not advance into ARM/BLA. Concretely: F ANK H; A CON H; A SMY H. I intend to fulfill the promises to Russia

[S1901M] I held to my promises: all three units remained defensive to reassure Russia and neighbors. Russia likewise honored its understanding — Sevastopol and southern Black Sea approaches were quiet and Moscow moved only northward to Livonia, not toward the Balkans or Constantinople. Italy respected our maritime understanding by moving into the Ionian/Tunis area. No power attacked my coasts or threatened the straits; Austria remained passive in the Balkans. No betrayals this turn. Impact: my borders and control of the straits are intact, giving time to build diplomacy and possibly a fleet later. Trust update: Russia and Italy are reliable for now; Austria cautious but not aggressive. Lesson: keep public, verifiable holds to lock in guarantees and insist on clear DMZ markers next turn to prevent surprise shifts.

support.order: F STP/SC S A MOS - LVN
